skill.name: skill-development
skill.description: Guides developers through creating, organizing, validating, and improving Claude Code plugin skills with clear trigger phrases and progressive disclosure.
skill.summary: Create and improve Claude Code plugin skills with structured guidance for content, resources, validation, and iteration.
skill.author: anthropics

security_audit.summary: Official Anthropic source reviewed with minimal audit scope. The only risk indicators are instructional examples for creating plugin skill folders and optional scripts; no malicious behavior or prompt injection attempt was found.

content.value_statement: Creating useful AI skills requires clear triggers, focused instructions, and reusable resources. This skill provides a repeatable process for designing, writing, validating, and improving Claude Code plugin skills.
actual_capabilities: Explains the required structure of Claude Code plugin skills and SKILL.md frontmatter.
Guides users through collecting concrete examples before writing a skill.
Helps decide when to add scripts, references, examples, or assets.
Provides writing guidance for trigger phrases and imperative skill instructions.
Defines validation checks for skill structure, references, examples, and scripts.
Supports iterative improvement after a skill is tested on real tasks.
limitations: Does not generate a complete marketplace listing by itself.
Does not include executable validation scripts in this package.
Requires user-provided domain examples for specialized skills.
Focuses on Claude Code plugin skills, not every external agent framework.

use_cases.title: Create a New Plugin Skill
use_cases.description: Plan the skill purpose, directory structure, trigger phrases, and reusable resources before writing SKILL.md.

use_cases.title: Improve Existing Skill Quality
use_cases.description: Refine descriptions, move detailed material into references, and validate progressive disclosure.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Skill Reviews
use_cases.description: Use the validation checklist to review structure, writing style, references, examples, and scripts.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Basic Skill
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner prompt for creating a simple plugin skill.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create a Claude Code plugin skill for [task]. Ask only the most important questions first, then propose the SKILL.md structure.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Reusable Resources
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate prompt for deciding what belongs in bundled resources.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these skill use cases: [examples]. Identify which scripts, references, examples, or assets would make the skill more reliable.

prompt_templates.title: Refine Skill Instructions
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate prompt for improving an existing SKILL.md file.
prompt_templates.prompt: Improve this SKILL.md for trigger quality, imperative writing style, progressive disclosure, and concise resource references.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Mature Skill
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt for validating a skill before release.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this plugin skill as a release candidate. Check structure, frontmatter, trigger phrases, references, examples, scripts, and iteration risks.

output_examples.input: Create a skill for validating release notes.
output_examples.output: A proposed skill plan with trigger phrases, SKILL.md sections, and a reference file for release note rules.

output_examples.input: Improve this existing plugin skill description.
output_examples.output: A clearer description with concrete user phrases, tighter scope, and a short summary of remaining gaps.

output_examples.input: Check whether this skill is ready to publish.
output_examples.output: A release readiness review covering structure, writing style, resource references, validation steps, and missing examples.

best_practices: Start from concrete user examples before designing the skill contents.
Keep SKILL.md concise and move detailed material into reference files.
Validate trigger phrases, referenced files, examples, and scripts before release.
anti_patterns: Writing vague descriptions that do not match real user requests.
Putting every detail in SKILL.md instead of using progressive disclosure.
Keeping generated example files that are not used by the final skill.

faq.question: What does this skill help me build?
faq.answer: It helps create and improve Claude Code plugin skills with clear structure, triggers, resources, and validation steps.

faq.question: Does it create files automatically?
faq.answer: It gives file structure guidance and command examples, but this package does not include executable creation scripts.

faq.question: Who should use this skill?
faq.answer: Developers, maintainers, and team leads who design or review Claude Code plugin skills should use it.

faq.question: How does it improve skill quality?
faq.answer: It emphasizes concrete examples, specific trigger phrases, lean instructions, reusable resources, and validation checks.

faq.question: Can it help update an existing skill?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes an iteration workflow for refining SKILL.md, references, bundled resources, and validation coverage.

faq.question: Is this limited to Claude Code plugins?
faq.answer: The guidance is optimized for Claude Code plugin skills, although some design principles apply to other AI skill systems.
